typeDecl.description: Declares one node type or one edge (relation) type in the vocabulary. For edge types: inverse/symmetric/transitive/reflexive/functional record the relation's algebra; domain/range are ASSERTED selectional preferences (the source/target node types the relation is intended to relate); domainIncludes/rangeIncludes are OBSERVED, non-exhaustive witnesses collected from the instance scene graphs — the true domain/codomain may be a supertype or a sum (union) of the witnessed types, so these are defeasible evidence, not hard constraints. sameAs aligns the term to external authorities (Wikidata, WordNet, SKOS).

id.description: Local term id, unique within this ontology.

domain.description: For edge types: ASSERTED allowed source node-type ids (selectional preference).

range.description: For edge types: ASSERTED allowed target node-type ids (selectional preference).

domainIncludes.description: For edge types: OBSERVED source node-type witnesses from instance edges (non-exhaustive; the real domain may be a supertype or a sum of these). Not a hard constraint.

rangeIncludes.description: For edge types: OBSERVED target node-type witnesses from instance edges (non-exhaustive; see domainIncludes).

inverse.description: For edge types: id of the CONVERSE edge type -- swap the arguments (A rel B <-> B inverse A), e.g. part-of <-> has-part, above <-> below. A symmetric relation is its own inverse.

opposite.description: For edge types: id of the directional ANTONYM -- SAME arguments, reversed path/orientation (e.g. into <-> out-of, up <-> down). Distinct from inverse, which swaps the arguments.

symmetric.description: For edge types: A rel B implies B rel A.

transitive.description: For edge types: A rel B and B rel C implies A rel C.

reflexive.description: For edge types: A rel A always holds.

functional.description: For edge types: each source relates to at most one target.

main.description: An open, shareable, forkable VOCABULARY (the typed-multigraph schema) for scene graphs: `nodeTypes` are its concept types and `edgeTypes` are its relation types (each carrying algebra, asserted domain/range, and observed domainIncludes/rangeIncludes witnesses). It deliberately does NOT assert type-to-type relation EDGES: a relation observed between two instances is an existential token (the instances are its witnesses) and licenses only a soft domain/codomain witness on the relation type, never a type-level relational axiom -- lifting it would be quantifier promotion (cf. the KL-ONE terminological/assertional split). The instance layer (scene.node/scene.edge) carries actual relations; this record is the schema they instantiate. Types and relations are data here, not baked into a lexicon, so any ontology (Visual Genome, WordNet, a research schema) can be expressed. schemaVersion pins the theory identity for migration.

schemaVersion.description: Identity/version of the underlying panproto theory this vocabulary realizes.

nodeTypes.description: The concept (node) types in the vocabulary.

edgeTypes.description: The relation (edge) types in the vocabulary, with algebra and domain/codomain (asserted preferences + observed witnesses).

imports.description: Other ontologies composed into this one.
